title: Crypto-D4 -- Choose and freeze ONE first crypto strategy spec (memo only)

1_data_readiness_context.qa: CLEAN: 0 dup, 0 invalid OHLC, 0 zero/neg vol, 0 missing calendar days; 2026 sealed out
1_data_readiness_context.data_used_here: metadata/read-only only (row counts, ranges, hashes from MANIFEST/provenance); NO bars were loaded, scanned, or backtested

2_candidate_comparison.name: Daily trend-following / Donchian-style breakout
2_candidate_comparison.why_edge: Crypto reflexive bubbles + retail FOMO produce fat-tailed sustained trends; momentum is the most documented crypto anomaly.
2_candidate_comparison.why_fail: Whipsaw in chop; breakouts fail in ranging regimes; crowded/decayed.
2_candidate_comparison.top_winner_dependence_risk: HIGH -- trend P&L concentrated in a few huge winners; this is the exact killer that PARKED every futures branch.
2_candidate_comparison.too_close_to_old_rejected: VERY close -- old crypto Donchian breakout (D2 inventory) + futures Donchian S23-S25 / S26-S29 trend family already PARKED.
2_candidate_comparison.entry_significance_testable: Partially -- trend edge often lives in exits/holding, frequently fails random-entry.
2_candidate_comparison.verdict: REJECT as first pick (highest top-winner risk; reuses most-rejected family).

2_candidate_comparison.name: Mean reversion after a large down day inside an uptrend
2_candidate_comparison.hypothesis: In an established uptrend, an outsized 1-day drop is an overreaction that reverts over the next few days.
2_candidate_comparison.why_edge: Crypto retail panic produces sharp liquidation overshoots; short-term reversal after extreme daily moves is documented; the uptrend filter aligns entries with the dominant crypto drift.
2_candidate_comparison.why_fail: A dip can be the start of a bear regime ('falling knife'); 2022 bear punishes it (uptrend filter mitigates); friction can eat a small per-trade edge.
2_candidate_comparison.expected_trade_count: MODERATE-TO-HIGH (dips in uptrends are recurrent) -> better statistical power, lower small-N risk. (A-priori expectation only; NOT measured -- measuring = backtest, forbidden here.)
2_candidate_comparison.friction_sensitivity: Moderate-high (more trades, smaller per-trade edge) -- a real risk, but explicitly testable under the friction gate.
2_candidate_comparison.top_winner_dependence_risk: LOWER -- many small mean-reversion wins capped by a fixed holding horizon, rather than a few giant winners. Directly attacks the universal killer.
2_candidate_comparison.multi_asset_testability: Good -- BTC/ETH/SOL all have uptrends + dips; identical-spec corroboration feasible.
2_candidate_comparison.too_close_to_old_rejected: Adjacent but distinct: ema_pullback (D2 item #2) is trend-CONTINUATION (buy strength in a pullback); this is a quantified shock-REVERSAL entry. Shares 'buy a dip in an uptrend' DNA -- flagged honestly -- but the trigger and intent differ.
2_candidate_comparison.entry_significance_testable: VERY clean -- the trigger is a discrete event and the holding horizon is FIXED, so a fixed-horizon random-entry permutation null is well defined.
2_candidate_comparison.data_requirements: Daily OHLC + a 200d SMA trend filter -- all derivable from the frozen daily closes.
2_candidate_comparison.verdict: SELECT -- best aligned with the factory's hard-won lesson (minimize top-winner dependence, maximize N, cleanest entry-significance test).

2_candidate_comparison.name: Volatility compression -> expansion breakout
2_candidate_comparison.hypothesis: Low-vol consolidation (squeeze) precedes large expansion moves.
2_candidate_comparison.why_edge: Volatility clustering is real; squeezes precede expansion.
2_candidate_comparison.why_fail: Expansion direction is undefined -> needs a directional overlay -> more parameters; harder to specify cleanly.
2_candidate_comparison.entry_significance_testable: Harder -- squeeze threshold + direction is a two-parameter entry, less clean.
2_candidate_comparison.verdict: REJECT as first pick (more parameter freedom -> overfit risk; weaker clean-entry test).

2_candidate_comparison.name: Cross-asset momentum confirmation (BTC leads ETH/SOL)
2_candidate_comparison.hypothesis: BTC moves lead ETH/SOL; trade alts when BTC confirms.
2_candidate_comparison.why_fail: Daily lead-lag is weak/near-contemporaneous (any lead is likely intraday, invisible on daily candles).
2_candidate_comparison.multi_asset_testability: WEAK paradox -- corroboration is baked INTO the signal, so BTC/ETH/SOL can no longer serve as independent robustness checks.
2_candidate_comparison.verdict: REJECT as first pick (daily lead-lag likely too weak; undermines the multi-asset robustness gate).

2_candidate_comparison.name: Weekend/weekday seasonality (day-of-week tendency)
2_candidate_comparison.hypothesis: 24/7 crypto with thin weekend liquidity produces a systematic calendar tendency.
2_candidate_comparison.why_fail: Tiny per-trade edge, friction-dominated; well-known and arbitraged; likely decayed/gone post-2021.
2_candidate_comparison.friction_sensitivity: VERY HIGH -- small calendar edges die to fees+slippage.
2_candidate_comparison.verdict: REJECT as first pick (likely FAIL for the wrong reason -- friction-dominated/decayed -- a poor first test).

2_candidate_comparison.name: Regime filter / no-trade overlay only
2_candidate_comparison.hypothesis: A regime classifier gates when (not whether) to trade.
2_candidate_comparison.why_edge: Regime awareness can suppress bad-regime trades.
2_candidate_comparison.why_fail: An overlay has NO entry of its own -- it is not a standalone strategy.
2_candidate_comparison.too_close_to_old_rejected: Old regime-HMM study (D2 item #3) is HISTORICAL_ONLY, holdout never closed.
2_candidate_comparison.verdict: EXCLUDE as a first STRATEGY (S30-D1 + Crypto-D1: an overlay is not a strategy). Reserve as a future overlay on top of a validated entry.

3_selection.selection_rationale: The universal killer across this factory is top-winner dependence (it parked every futures branch). Candidate 2 minimizes that (fixed-horizon caps winner size, many small trades), delivers the highest expected trade count (statistical power), has the cleanest fixed-horizon entry-significance test, strong identical-spec BTC/ETH/SOL corroboration, daily-spot-only data needs, and is distinct from the already-parked trend-continuation family. Candidates 1 and 5 are likely to FAIL for structural reasons (top-winner concentration / friction-domination); 3 and 4 add parameter freedom or undercut the robustness gate; 6 is not a standalone strategy.

4_frozen_spec.strategy_name: CODR-1 (Crypto Oversold-Dip Reversion in Uptrend) v1
4_frozen_spec.direction: LONG ONLY (spot; no shorting, no leverage)
4_frozen_spec.data_source: frozen snapshot spot_binance_usdt_1d_2020_2025 (hashes pinned in section 1); read-only

IS.SOLUSDT: 2020-08-11..2023-12-31 (PARTIAL IS -- ~4.4 months short of 2020 start; reduced power; reported separately, NOT padded or stitched)

OOS.all: 2024-01-01..2025-12-31 (SEALED, one-shot after a committed protocol hash in a later step)

windows.2026: SEALED OUT (absent from the snapshot; blocked unless separately authorized)

frozen_parameters_no_sweep.trend_filter: close_t > SMA200_t (200-day simple moving average of close); canonical, not tuned
frozen_parameters_no_sweep.shock_trigger: daily close-to-close return ret_t = close_t/close_{t-1} - 1 <= -0.07 (a -7% or worse down day); single pre-registered constant
frozen_parameters_no_sweep.entry_timing: enter LONG at the next bar's OPEN (open_{t+1}) when BOTH close_t > SMA200_t AND ret_t <= -0.07 (signal uses only data through close_t; fill at open_{t+1}; no look-ahead)
frozen_parameters_no_sweep.position_rule: one open position per symbol; NO pyramiding -- ignore new triggers while a position is open
frozen_parameters_no_sweep.protective_stop: if a daily close <= entry_fill * 0.90 (-10%), exit at THAT day's close (close-based, to avoid unrealistic intrabar fills on daily data)
frozen_parameters_no_sweep.time_stop: if not stopped, exit at the CLOSE of the 5th daily bar after entry (fixed 5-bar holding horizon)
frozen_parameters_no_sweep.exit_priority: first of {protective_stop, time_stop} to trigger wins
frozen_parameters_no_sweep.profit_target: NONE in v1 -- the fixed 5-bar horizon intentionally caps winner size (reduces top-winner dependence) and keeps the random-entry null clean; adding a target is a future variant, not v1

4_frozen_spec.position_accounting: fixed notional per trade, no leverage, no compounding within the test (clean per-trade R); cash account; per-trade return = exit_price/entry_price - 1 - costs; results reported in % and in R
look_ahead_guards: SMA200 and ret use only data through close_t
fills at open_{t+1} or at a later bar's close
no use of same-bar future high/low for fills

5_validation_ladder: Crypto-D5: implement engine + tests only (NO run)
Crypto-D7: OOS protocol pre-registration (committed protocol hash)
Crypto-D8: OOS once (one-shot, no post-OOS tuning)
Crypto-D10: sequence risk / Monte Carlo (trade-order shuffle + bootstrap + top-winner removal)
Crypto-D12: multi-asset crypto robustness (identical spec on BTC/ETH/SOL)
Crypto-D15: final decision gate (one research decision + one readiness level; PAPER/LIVE unreachable on default path)

6_gates.enough_trades: >= 30 trades per symbol on IS (else WATCH/insufficient-power; SOL partial-IS expected to be thinner -> flagged, not hidden)
6_gates.top3_winner_dependence: HARD GATE -- net P&L stays positive after removing the top 3 winners (the gate that parked every futures branch)
6_gates.sequence_risk: Monte Carlo trade-order shuffle + bootstrap; 5th-percentile terminal P&L not catastrophic; edge not path-luck
6_gates.friction: survives the ~0.30% base round-trip AND a +50% friction stress
6_gates.multi_asset_corroboration: same-sign positive expectancy on >= 2 of 3 symbols; edge not confined to one coin
6_gates.no_promotion: NO live/paper promotion regardless of result
6_gates.labels: PASS = survives all gates on IS -> proceed to OOS pre-registration (research only). WATCH = mixed/thin -> park as candidate. FAIL = stop the branch.

entry_significance_protocol: Fixed-horizon random-entry permutation: hold the symbol, trade count, fixed 5-bar horizon, and the -10% stop constant; replace the real entry dates with N random eligible entry dates; build the null distribution of mean per-trade return; the real edge must beat the 95th percentile of the random-entry null. The FIXED holding horizon is what makes this null clean.
sequence_risk_protocol: Trade-order shuffle (Monte Carlo) + bootstrap resampling of the per-trade return series; report terminal-P&L and max-drawdown distributions; top-winner-removal sensitivity (drop top 1 / 3 / 5 winners).
multi_asset_robustness_rules: Run the IDENTICAL frozen spec (no per-symbol tuning) on BTC/ETH/SOL; report each symbol separately; SOL partial-IS power reported honestly; edge must not be confined to a single coin.
anti_overfit_rules: All parameters are single pre-registered constants (SMA200, -7% trigger, 5-bar horizon, -10% stop, 0.10% fee, 0.05% slippage) -- NO ranges, NO sweep, NO grid search
Identical spec across all three symbols (no per-symbol parameter tuning)
One-shot OOS after a committed protocol hash; NO tuning after OOS
NO parameter import from any old crypto work (D2 historical-only)
NO post-hoc threshold adjustment after seeing any result
Any change to any constant = a NEW spec id and a fresh ladder, not an edit to CODR-1 v1
8_final_line: Crypto-D4 is a frozen strategy-spec step only; no crypto backtest, OOS, exchange API, paper trading, or live trading is authorized.
trading_recommendation: NONE. Spec/memo only. One first crypto strategy (CODR-1: long-only oversold-dip reversion inside an uptrend, daily BTC/ETH/SOL spot USDT) is now pre-registered and frozen for future testing, but it has NOT been coded, backtested, IS/OOS-run, or validated; no crypto strategy is paper-ready or live-ready; perps remain blocked; crypto stays a separate research lane; S30 and the futures branches are untouched and S30 remains PARKED after IS_FAIL.
